name: Code duplication follows community conventions
description: The code duplication ratio is maintained at a reasonable level according to community's standards and conventions. Code duplication ratio is the percentage of code within a code base or project that is syntactically identical, often measured using code blocks. An example for calculating it may be (lines_duplicated/lines_of_code)*100.

source.name: Definition, approaches, and analysis of code duplication detection (2006–2020): a critical review
